notes: Manual addition (Gabe-requested) 2026-06-20: a $1000 Roman-mythology deep cut — the Roman counterpart of Nike. The Greek name is globally famous (sportswear), but the Roman name (Victoria) is far more familiar as a personal name than as a deity. Grounded in the Victoria (mythology) Wikipedia lead.

questions.clue: First appearing during the First Punic War as a Roman renaming of the Greek goddess Nike, this deified personification of triumph had — unlike her Greek counterpart — virtually no mythology of her own; her name is now far better known as a given name, a long-reigning British queen, and a Canadian provincial capital.

citations.title: Victoria (mythology)
citations.snippet: In ancient Roman religion Victoria was the deified personification of victory. She first appeared during the first Punic War, seemingly as a Romanised re-naming of Nike, the goddess of victory associated with Rome's Greek allies in the Gree
